macro_segments.title: Introduction to derivation rules and limitations in kodshim

micro_segments.title: Overview of special rules for learning halachos of kodshim and the principle of me'akev
The mesora of chazal regarding details of sacrifices and the requirement of shina lo hakatuv le'akeiv
How details are learned and applied across different korbanot through connections and derivations
The fundamental principle that you can do lemadim lamad milamad (derivation from derivation) throughout Torah
Limitations on derivations specifically by korbanot and the principle of davar halamad behekkesh ein chozer umelamed behekkesh
Illustration of the hekkesh min hahekkesh rule with the example of chulin and asham learning tzafon from olah
Second limitation: gezeirah shavah cannot be built on hekkesh; the case of chatas and asham
Overview of the 4x4 matrix of derivation methods: hekkesh, gezeirah shavah, binyan av, kal vachomer in both directions

macro_segments.title: Hekkesh as first method: deriving to hekkesh, gezeirah shavah, kal vachomer, and binyan av

micro_segments.title: Davar halamad mehekkesh chozer umelamed bekal vachomer: hekkesh followed by kal vachomer is permitted
Evidence from Tanna D'vei Rabbi Yishmael: applying rules of inner chatas to Par Yom Kippur via hekkesh and kal vachomer
Davar halamad behekkesh mahu sheyilamed bbinyan av: debate about hekkesh followed by binyan av
Amar Rabbi Yirmiah: the Torah explicitly says tzafon by asham, showing binyan av cannot be built on hekkesh
Gemara challenges: why didn't Torah apply the hekkesh from olah to chatas via binyan av; analysis of what makes a valid binyan av
Why olah, chatas, and asham each require explicit statements: differences make binyan av invalid (olah is entirely burned, chatas is mechaper, asham is lighter)
Gemara concludes: binyan av failure here is not about hekkesh first, but about intrinsic problems with the binyan av itself
Gemara's question: could the three-way paradigm (olah, chatas, asham) have created a common category without needing explicit statement by each
Analysis of why two-source binyan av cannot work: olah burns completely (unlike chatas/asham); chatas achieves kapparah (unlike olah/asham); asham applies to different categories of obligation

macro_segments.title: Gezeirah shavah as first method: deriving to hekkesh, gezeirah shavah, kal vachomer, and binyan av

micro_segments.title: Davar halamad bigzeirah shavah mahu sheyilamed behekkesh: case of todah from ma'aser via shlamim and sham sham
Learning sequence: ma'aser to shlamim via gezeirah shavah of sham, then shlamim to todah via hekkesh
Rav Pappa's position: you can do gezeirah shavah then hekkesh, but debate about whether restrictions apply to source or application
Mar Zutra challenges: ma'aser is chulin, so restrictions of kodshim should not apply when learning from chulin sources
Rav Pappa responds: restrictions apply to what you're applying it to (the derived law), not where you're coming from (the source)
Rashi's interpretation: underlying dispute about whether restrictions follow the source (melamed) or the application (lamed)
Davar halamad bigzeirah shavah mahu sheyilamed bigzeirah shavah: gezeirah shavah to gezeirah shavah via case of breads with todah
Rami bar Chama's case: soles, chalos, rekikin all require fine flour via double gezeirah shavah of chalos and matzos
Ravina challenges: maybe matzos-matzos is from a direct source (mincha of todah), not from the chalos that was itself learned via gezeirah shavah
Rava's alternative source: explicit teaching about cutting up the inner chatas and applying to Yom Kippur via gezeirah shavah of eretz-basar-perish
Davar halamad bigzeirah shavah mahu sheyilamed bekal vachomer: gezeirah shavah followed by kal vachomer

Logical proof using kal vachomer: hekkesh is weaker (can't do hekkesh-hekkesh) but permits kal vachomer; gezeirah shavah is stronger (permits gezeirah shavah-gezeirah shavah); so gezeirah shavah should certainly permit kal vachomer
Gemara accepts the logic: if hekkesh permits kal vachomer and gezeirah shavah permits hekkesh, gezeirah shavah permits kal vachomer
Davar halamad bigzeirah shavah mahu sheyilamed bbinyan av: gezeirah shavah followed by binyan av—teiku (unresolved)

macro_segments.title: Kal vachomer as first method: deriving to hekkesh, gezeirah shavah, kal vachomer, and binyan av

micro_segments.title: Davar halamad bekal vachomer mahu sheyilamed behekkesh: kal vachomer followed by hekkesh
Davar halamad bekal vachomer mahu sheyilamed bigzeirah shavah: kal vachomer followed by gezeirah shavah
Logical argument: kal vachomer is stronger than gezeirah shavah (permits hekkesh while gezeirah shavah cannot); therefore if gezeirah shavah permits hekkesh (Rav Pappa), kal vachomer certainly does

Logical proof: gezeirah shavah cannot build on hekkesh (weaker), but can build on gezeirah shavah (itself); kal vachomer is stronger than gezeirah shavah; therefore kal vachomer permits gezeirah shavah
Gemara limits the proof: only works if you accept Rav Pappa's position on gezeirah shavah-hekkesh; otherwise teiku
Davar halamad bekal vachomer mahu sheyilamed bekal vachomer: kal vachomer followed by kal vachomer
First attempt using kal vachomer: gezeirah shavah cannot build on hekkesh but can build on kal vachomer; therefore kal vachomer permits kal vachomer
Gemara's objection: this proof uses kal vachomer to prove kal vachomer, making it kal vachomer ben kal vachomer (grandson), which begs the question
Alternative proof without using derived kal vachomer: hekkesh cannot build on hekkesh (weak) but permits kal vachomer (strong); therefore kal vachomer certainly permits kal vachomer
Davar halamad bekal vachomer mahu sheyilamed bbinyan av: kal vachomer followed by binyan av
Rav Yirmiah's case: malikat and tereifah bird; Rebbi Meir uses kal vachomer to show shechitatah protects against tumah, then binyan av to apply same to melikat
Rebbi Meir's kal vachomer: behemah nevela is more stringent (metamei in touching and carrying); bird nevela is less stringent (only metamei in swallowing); so shechitatah certainly permits bird
Binyan av built on kal vachomer: shechita maksheret be'achilatah umetaheret tereifatah; therefore melikat which also maksheret should also protect from tumah
Gemara's objection: the case only works because it starts from chulin (behemah and of), so restrictions of kodshim do not apply; we do not know if kal vachomer permits binyan av within kodshim

topical_tags.term: shina lo hakatuv le'akeiv (Torah must repeat to make essential)
davar halamad behekkesh ein chozer umelamed behekkesh (hekkesh cannot derive hekkesh)
kal vachomer (logical inference from weaker to stronger)
Par He'elam Davar (bull of the anointed priest)
inner chatas of kohen mashiach / Kohen Gadol
